paper_title: RobustAD: Real-World Robustness in Industrial Anomaly Detection
task_concept: Build a robust anomaly detection system that maintains performance under domain shifts. Given the RobustAD dataset (3 sub-datasets: PCB, metal parts, piled packets), you must: (1) implement a standard anomaly detection method (PatchCore), (2) train on the source domain (clean images) for each sub-dataset, (3) evaluate on multiple target domains with different shifts (lighting, pose, background clutter), (4) implement the Average Relative Drop metric that measures performance degradation across shifts, (5) implement a simple domain adaptation technique (e.g., input normalization or style transfer) to improve robustness, and (6) compare adapted vs non-adapted performance showing improvement in Average Relative Drop.
suggested_harbor_steps:
1. data-and-setup: Download RobustAD, load 3 sub-datasets; Train on source domain per sub-dataset.
2. model-development: Implement PatchCore anomaly detector; Add domain adaptation technique.
3. evaluation-and-reporting: Evaluate on all target domains; Implement Average Relative Drop; Compare adapted vs non-adapted.
verifier_ideas: PatchCore implemented; Training complete; Multi-target eval done; Avg Relative Drop computed; Domain adaptation added; Improvement shown
